Tax Treatment Options

Capital Gains (Recommended)
Form 8949 with FIFO cost basis calculation
Gambling Income
Schedule 1, Line 8b reporting
Other Income
Schedule 1, Line 8z reporting

Common Questions About Polymarket Tax Reporting

Yes, all earnings from Polymarket prediction markets must be reported to the IRS. This includes both winning predictions and any market-making profits. The IRS considers these taxable events that need to be properly documented.

No, Polymarket does not currently issue 1099 forms to users. As a decentralized prediction market, they don't collect the tax information needed to generate these forms. That's why you need to track and report your transactions independently using tools like PolyTax.

Calculate your gain or loss by subtracting your cost basis (purchase price) from your proceeds (sale price) for each position. PolyTax automatically calculates this for all your trades and generates the required IRS forms with accurate calculations.

You'll need IRS Form 8949 to report your individual Polymarket transactions and Schedule D to summarize your capital gains and losses. PolyTax generates both forms automatically from your Polymarket trading history.

Most Polymarket trades are taxed as short-term capital gains (held less than one year) at your ordinary income tax rate. Positions held over one year may qualify for long-term capital gains rates. Market-making activities might be considered ordinary income.

PolyTax automatically tracks your USDC basis and converts all transactions to USD values using historical exchange rates. This ensures accurate reporting even when trading with stablecoins.

Yes, capital losses from Polymarket can offset capital gains from other investments. If losses exceed gains, you can deduct up to $3,000 against ordinary income, with excess losses carried forward to future years.

For 2024 earnings: Returns were due April 15, 2025 (October 15 with extension). For 2025 earnings: Quarterly estimated payments are due June 17, Sept 16, and Jan 15, 2026. The full 2025 return is due April 15, 2026. If you have substantial gains, paying quarterly helps avoid underpayment penalties.

Yes! If you know other Polymarket traders who need tax documents, you can share PolyTax with them. We have a referral program that pays $36 for each person who uses the service through your link. It's a simple way to help your trading community while earning some extra income. Get your referral link here.
